rationale: Both papers focus on the U.S. motion picture market and directly overlap on core constructs: advertising, word-of-mouth/information effects, and opening vs postrelease box-office dynamics. The citing paper’s stated contribution is to extend prior movie diffusion/advertising work, while the cited paper provides empirical evidence on how advertising signals affect opening and later revenues in this same setting, making it a plausible foundational/comparison source rather than an obvious incidental mention.
